metadata.title: Voices: Free vs Cloned
metadata.audience: Humanitarians AI Fellows learning to use the Brutalist pipeline

beats.narration_text: Voices: Free vs Cloned. Free by default, cloned by choice, gated always. Liam, in for Bear.

props.command: What voices can I use in Brutalist? What is Kokoro and how is it different from ElevenLabs?
output: Kokoro: free, local, no API key
ElevenLabs: Bear's clone, metered, GATE P

beats.narration_text: Two lanes. Left lane: Kokoro. Free, local, no API key, voice code am underscore onyx — that's the Liam voice you're hearing right now. Right lane: ElevenLabs. Bear's cloned voice, metered spend, and gated — a human approves before any ElevenLabs audio is generated.

props.sparkLine: Free by default. Cloned by choice. Gated always.

beats.narration_text: To use Kokoro, set voice underscore kokoro to am underscore onyx in your beat sheet metadata. That's the default for all HAI Fellows builds. Kokoro is Apache-licensed and runs entirely on your machine — no account, no quota, no cost. Near-real-time on an M1.

props.title: kokoro setup
props.code:
# In beat_sheet.json metadata:
"engine": "kokoro",
"voice_kokoro": "am_onyx",

# Generate audio:
python3 runtime/scripts/generate_audio_kokoro.py \
  brutalist-art/youtube/hai-brutalist-what-is --no-gate

beats.narration_text: The GATE P checkpoint is a deliberate friction point. ElevenLabs uses Bear's cloned voice, which sounds like a real person. The gate ensures a human reviews and approves the content before any spend is triggered. For all standard Fellows builds, Kokoro is the right choice — no gate needed.

artifactLines: ElevenLabs uses Bear's voice — a real person's clone.
GATE P requires human review before any spend is triggered.
Kokoro bypasses the gate — it costs nothing and risks nothing.
For all HAI Fellows builds: use Kokoro, --no-gate flag.
props.sparkLine: Free voice. No gate. No spend.

beats.narration_text: Free by default, cloned by choice, gated always. Use Kokoro am underscore onyx for all standard builds.

artifactLines: Kokoro am_onyx: free, local, Apache-licensed — the default.
ElevenLabs: Bear's clone, metered, requires GATE P approval.
Set engine: kokoro and voice_kokoro: am_onyx in metadata.
Run generate_audio_kokoro.py with --no-gate for all HAI builds.

beats.narration_text: Here's the prompt. My beat sheet uses engine: kokoro and voice underscore kokoro: am underscore onyx. How do I regenerate just beat B03's audio after editing the narration text, and how do I verify the new duration was written back? Run that prompt when you need to fix a single beat's audio. Episode nine covers making it move — that's next.

props.command: My beat sheet uses engine: kokoro and voice_kokoro: am_onyx. How do I regenerate just beat B03's audio after editing the narration text, and verify the new duration was written back?

beats.narration_text: Voices: Free vs Cloned. Episode eight of twelve. Next: Make It Move.
